title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup after 10 months with no minutes restriction, contributing to a 120-100 win over Mavericks.
Despite initial rust, Tatum scored 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists, receiving a huge ovation from the TD Garden crowd.
Amid Tatum's return, Celtics' Neemias Queta impressed with 16 points, 15 rebounds; Nikola Vucevic sidelined for a month with finger injury.

title: Why Bruins made right call to stand pat at trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins chose not to make major trades at the NHL trade deadline to preserve future assets.
General Manager Don Sweeney focused on long-term rebuilding instead of making short-term acquisitions for playoff push.
Bruins have four first-round picks over the next two years, emphasizing their commitment to future prospects.

title: Patriots reportedly miss out on Maxx Crosby trade
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Maxx Crosby is reportedly being traded from the Las Vegas Raiders to the Baltimore Ravens for two first-round draft picks.
Crosby, interested in the Patriots and Cowboys, will join the Ravens despite public denials of a possible trade.
The Patriots likely couldn't match the Ravens' offer due to having lower draft picks and high compensation demands.

title: Report: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic to miss one month with broken finger
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics center Nikola Vucevic will miss about a month with a fractured right ring finger sustained against the Mavericks.
Vucevic averaged 11.4 points and 8.8 rebounds for the Celtics since joining the team at the trade deadline.
Jayson Tatum returned from injury on Friday, while Neemias Queta and Luka Garza provide depth in Vucevic's absence.

title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics lineup 297 days after rupturing his Achilles tendon in the 2025 playoffs.
Fans at TD Garden gave Tatum a thunderous ovation as he was introduced before the game against the Dallas Mavericks.
Tatum played limited minutes, scoring his first points of the season with a dunk and a three-pointer in the second quarter.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots plan to trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, reported by ESPN.
Trade can't finalize until the NFL's new league year starts; Bears need a center after Drew Dalman's retirement.
Patriots may replace Bradbury with Jared Wilson, opening up $5.7 million in cap space for free agency.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hamnet' (2025), a fictionalized Shakespearean tale, is streaming on Peacock with Jessie Buckley and Paul Mescal.
'It Was Just an Accident' by Jafar Panahi, critiquing Iranian authority, is available on Hulu.
'Vladimir' (2026), a series featuring Rachel Weisz, explores college scandals and is streaming on Netflix.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics' starting lineup for his regular-season debut against the Mavericks.
Coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's work ethic and constant communication during his recovery journey.
Tatum replaced Baylor Scheierman, and there was no mention of a minutes restriction for his return.

title: Bruins trade for former first-round pick Lukas Reichel
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded a 2026 sixth-round pick to the Canucks for forward Lukas Reichel.
Lukas Reichel, a 23-year-old, was drafted 17th overall in the 2020 NHL Draft by Chicago.
Reichel scored two goals in 19 NHL games this season and will start with Providence in the AHL.

title: 2 Boston restaurants to pay  $400K for failure to pay service fees to staff, AGO alleges
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Carrie Nation Cocktail Club and The Dubliner must pay over $422,000 for mislabeling service fees, in a settlement with the AGO.
The fees, intended for service staff, were incorrectly recorded due to a point-of-sale system error, according to East Coast Tavern Group.
The restaurants charged a 3% 'service fee' from May 2023 to June 2024 but failed to distribute it to 84 employees.
